title: Monster Branch Reframe
subtitle: Review the rewrite of strategy and product artifacts around the monster branch causal chain: scope creep → branch bloat → review bottleneck → velocity trap → attention drain

items.title: .debussy/strategy/pitch.md — Problem Section
items.description: Replaced three independent pain points (P1/P2/P3) with a single causal chain narrative. P1/P2/P3 are now derived as “Consequences” of the monster branch loop. P3 renamed from “Solo builders do it all” to “Parallelism without visibility”.
items.details:
## The Problem: The Monster Branch

Claude Code can tackle anything you throw at it. And that’s exactly the trap.

You start a session with a clear intent. But Claude is fast, and you have ideas. You ask for one more thing, then another. The branch grows. Pretty soon, you’re sitting on a monster branch — massive local changes, four or five ambitious things tangled together in a mess.

You ask for a commit plan — you get one. But the resulting PR is still unreviewable: too broad for meaningful human or agent review. It does too many things, and reviewing it seriously starts to take longer than building it.

So you start planning and rescoping. But then you either lose velocity by working sequentially, or juggle complexity by working in parallel across sessions. Either way, your attention scatters, the artifacts you should be reviewing get lost, and the velocity drops.

This is the core loop Debussy breaks. And it leads naturally upstream: if you’re going to plan and scope work to avoid the monster branch, you might as well do it with as much context as you can.

### Consequences

**P1: Feedback lost, attention scattered**
Your feedback on key artifacts is scattered across conversation threads, never captured in a form you or the agent can reliably retrieve.

**P2: Documentation that agents need but humans can’t maintain**
Your agent needs documentation to work well. If you’re scoping work to avoid monster branches, you need this context — and it needs to be agent-maintainable.

**P3: Parallelism without visibility**
You launch a second session, then a third. Without discipline and tools to organize parallel work, you lose track.
items.context: Previously: three independent pain points (P1: Feedback lost, P2: Docs unmaintained, P3: Solo builders do it all) with no unifying narrative

items.title: .debussy/product/product.md — Positioning
items.description: Positioning rewritten to anchor in the monster branch loop. Each Debussy capability is framed as breaking a specific link in the chain.
items.details:
## Positioning
Claude Code’s raw capability creates a trap: the monster branch. You throw ideas at it, the branch grows, the PR becomes unreviewable, and you end up rescoping — losing the velocity you gained. Debussy breaks this loop at every link: strategy and product strates scope the work upstream with full context, engineering governance prevents scope creep, the inbox captures your feedback on artifacts where it counts, and parallel lanes make multi-session work visible and manageable.
items.context: Previously: "Solo builders carry every role. The goal is to automate where you can and review where your expertise matters..."

items.title: .debussy/product/intents.md — Chain Links
items.description: Added “Breaks chain at:” field to each intent, mapping it to the causal chain step it addresses. Updated P3 label. Added chain summary in preamble. Intents 010-012 included.
items.details:
New preamble: “Every intent is linked to the monster branch causal chain it breaks: scope creep → branch bloat → review bottleneck → velocity trap → attention drain.”

Chain mapping:
• 001 Product Strate → scope creep
• 002 Inbox Review UX → review bottleneck, attention drain
• 003 Workflow Monitoring → velocity trap
• 004 Parallel Lanes → velocity trap
• 005 Engineering Strate → branch bloat
• 006 Status Line → velocity trap, attention drain
• 007 Unified UI → attention drain
• 008 Strategy Depth → scope creep
• 009 Setup Explorer → velocity trap
• 010 Lane Lifecycle → branch bloat, velocity trap
• 011 Release Orchestration → review bottleneck
• 012 QA Staging Loop → review bottleneck, attention drain
items.context: Previously: intents only had “Addresses: P1/P2/P3” with no chain reference

items.title: docs/vision.md — Vision & Pain Points
items.description: Rewrote “Why We’re Building This” to lead with the monster branch. Replaced “Pain Points” section with “The Core Problem: The Monster Branch” + Consequences. Same narrative as pitch.md but public-facing (shorter consequence descriptions).
items.details:
## Why We’re Building This
Claude Code is the most-used AI coding agent on the market — $2.5B run-rate, 75% adoption at startups, 73% of engineering teams using AI tools daily. But its raw capability creates a trap that solo builders fall into daily: the monster branch. Debussy exists to break that loop — from scoping work upstream with full context, to making parallel execution visible and manageable.

## The Core Problem: The Monster Branch
[Same narrative as pitch.md]

### Consequences
P1, P2, P3 as compact one-liners (vs. full paragraphs in pitch.md)
items.context: Previously: generic “invisible friction” framing, three numbered pain points as independent sections

items.title: docs/product.md — One-Liner
items.description: One-liner updated from “disciplined approach from idea to shippable feature” to “breaks the monster branch loop”.
items.details:
Old: A Claude Code meta-plugin that gives solo builders a disciplined approach from idea to shippable feature — without losing the thread.

New: A Claude Code meta-plugin that breaks the monster branch loop — from scoping work upstream with full context to making parallel execution visible and manageable.
